---
title: "Analyse Stylométrique du XIXe siècle"
subtitle: "Projet 1 · PySpark · Spark NLP"
author: "Krista Carla Sevi · Mick-Wanderly Laveus · Leon Lafosse"
date: today
lang: fr
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    logo: ""
    footer: "Analyse Stylométrique XIXe — Big Data"
    highlight-style: github
    code-fold: true
    self-contained: true
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  error: false
  cache: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import os, re, math, time, warnings
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.types import (
    StringType, StructType, StructField,
    LongType, DoubleType, ArrayType, IntegerType
)
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

N_CORES = os.cpu_count() or 4

spark = (
    SparkSession.builder
    .appName("Stylometry19C")
    .master(f"local[{N_CORES}]")
    .config("spark.sql.shuffle.partitions", str(N_CORES))
    .config("spark.default.parallelism",    str(N_CORES))
    .config("spark.sql.adaptive.enabled",                       "true")
    .config("spark.sql.adaptive.coalescePartitions.enabled",    "true")
    .config("spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled",              "true")
    .config("spark.serializer",              "org.apache.spark.serializer.KryoSerializer")
    .config("spark.kryoserializer.buffer.max","512m")
    .config("spark.driver.memory",  "4g")
    .config("spark.executor.memory","4g")
    .getOrCreate()
)
spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("ERROR")

DATA_DIR    = Path("DATA")
PARQUET_DIR = Path("_outdir/parquet")
PARQUET_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

COLOR_COURANT = {"Romantisme": "#4C72B0", "Réalisme": "#DD8452"}
COLOR_AUTHOR = {
    "Balzac":         "#1f77b4",
    "Hugo":           "#aec7e8",
    "Stendhal":       "#2ca02c",
    "Sand":           "#98df8a",
    "Flaubert":       "#d62728",
    "Zola":           "#ff9896",
    "Voltaire":       "#9467bd",
    "Chateaubriand":  "#8c564b",
    "Gautier":        "#e377c2",
}
```

```{python}
#| label: metadata-and-etl
#| include: false

METADATA = {
    "Adieu.txt":                   ("Adieu",                           "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Albert-Savarus.txt":          ("Albert Savarus",                  "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Autre-etude-de-femme.txt":    ("Autre étude de femme",            "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Avant-propos.txt":            ("Avant-propos — Comédie humaine",  "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Beatrix.txt":                 ("Béatrix",                         "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Cesar-Birotteau.txt":         ("César Birotteau",                 "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Combien-l_amour-revient.txt": ("A combien l'amour revient",       "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Deux-jeunes-mariees.txt":     ("Memoires de deux jeunes mariees", "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "El-verdugo.txt":              ("El Verdugo",                      "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Esquisse-d_homme-d_affaires.txt": ("Esquisse d'homme d'affaires", "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Esther-heureuse.txt":             ("Esther heureuse",             "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Etude-de-femme.txt":              ("Etude de femme",              "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Eugenie-Grandet.txt":             ("Eugenie Grandet",             "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Eve-et-David.txt":                ("Eve et David",                "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Facino-Cane.txt":                 ("Facino Cane",                 "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Ferragus.txt":                    ("Ferragus",                    "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Gambara.txt":                     ("Gambara",                     "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Gaudissart-II.txt":               ("Gaudissart II",               "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Gobseck.txt":                     ("Gobseck",                     "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Honorine.txt":                    ("Honorine",                    "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "Jesus-Christ-en-Flandre.txt":     ("Jesus-Christ en Flandre",    "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "La-bourse.txt":                   ("La Bourse",                   "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "La-cousine-Bette.txt":            ("La Cousine Bette",            "Balzac", "Réalisme"),
    "hugo_ndp.txt":                ("Notre-Dame de Paris",             "Hugo",   "Romantisme"),
    "hugo_qvt.txt":                ("Quatrevingt-Treize",              "Hugo",   "Romantisme"),
    "hugo_ldj.txt":                ("Le Dernier Jour d'un Condamne",   "Hugo",   "Romantisme"),
    "stendhal_le_rouge_et_le_noir.txt":    ("Le Rouge et le Noir",       "Stendhal", "Réalisme"),
    "stendhal_la_chartreuse_de_parme.txt": ("La Chartreuse de Parme",    "Stendhal", "Réalisme"),
    "stendhal_souvenirs_d_egoisme.txt":    ("Souvenirs d'egotisme",      "Stendhal", "Réalisme"),
    "stendhal_de_l_amour.txt":             ("De l'Amour",                "Stendhal", "Réalisme"),
    "sand_lpf.txt":                ("La Petite Fadette",               "Sand",   "Romantisme"),
    "sand_gabriel.txt":            ("Gabriel",                         "Sand",   "Romantisme"),
    "flaubert_madame_bovary.txt":       ("Madame Bovary",        "Flaubert", "Réalisme"),
    "flaubert_bouvard_et_pecuchet.txt": ("Bouvard et Pecuchet",  "Flaubert", "Réalisme"),
    "flaubert_trois_contes.txt":        ("Trois Contes",          "Flaubert", "Réalisme"),
    "flaubert_un_coeur_simple.txt":     ("Un Coeur simple",       "Flaubert", "Réalisme"),
    "Zola_germinal.txt":           ("Germinal",                       "Zola",   "Réalisme"),
    "Zola_l_assomoir.txt":         ("L'Assommoir",                    "Zola",   "Réalisme"),
    "Zola_lourdes.txt":            ("Lourdes",                        "Zola",   "Réalisme"),
    "Maupassant_candide.txt":      ("Candide",                        "Voltaire", "Romantisme"),
    "chateaubriand_vie_de_rance.txt": ("Vie de Rance",               "Chateaubriand", "Romantisme"),
    "gautier_clarimonde.txt":      ("La Morte amoureuse (Clarimonde)", "Gautier", "Romantisme"),
    "gautier_emau.txt":            ("Emaux et Camees",                 "Gautier", "Romantisme"),
    "gautier_menagerie_intime.txt":("Menagerie intime",                "Gautier", "Romantisme"),
    "gautier_sacountala.txt":      ("Sacountala",                      "Gautier", "Romantisme"),
}

START_PAT = re.compile(r"\*{3}\s*START OF[^\*]*\*{3}", re.IGNORECASE)
END_PAT   = re.compile(r"\*{3}\s*END OF[^\*]*\*{3}",   re.IGNORECASE)
ORNAMENT  = re.compile(r"^\s*[\*\-\=]{3,}\s*$", re.MULTILINE)
ROMAN_CH  = re.compile(
    r"^\s*(M{0,3})(CM|CD|D?C{0,3})(XC|XL|L?X{0,3})(IX|IV|V?I{0,3})\s*\.?\s*$",
    re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE
)

def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
    m = START_PAT.search(text)
    if m: text = text[m.end():]
    m = END_PAT.search(text)
    if m: text = text[:m.start()]
    text = ORNAMENT.sub("", text)
    text = ROMAN_CH.sub("", text)
    return text.strip()

UPLOAD_DIR = Path("/mnt/user-data/uploads")

records = []
for fname, (title, author, courant) in METADATA.items():
    candidates = [DATA_DIR / fname, UPLOAD_DIR / fname]
    path = next((p for p in candidates if p.exists()), None)
    if path is None:
        continue
    raw     = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    cleaned = clean_text(raw)
    records.append((fname, title, author, courant, cleaned))

schema = StructType([
    StructField("filename", StringType(), False),
    StructField("title",    StringType(), False),
    StructField("author",   StringType(), False),
    StructField("courant",  StringType(), False),
    StructField("text",     StringType(), False),
])

corpus_sdf = spark.createDataFrame(records, schema)
corpus_sdf.cache()
N_DOCS = corpus_sdf.count()

meta_rows   = [(f, t, a, c) for f,(t,a,c) in METADATA.items()]
meta_schema = StructType([
    StructField("filename", StringType()),
    StructField("title",    StringType()),
    StructField("author",   StringType()),
    StructField("courant",  StringType()),
])
df_meta = spark.createDataFrame(meta_rows, meta_schema)
```

```{python}
#| label: stats-base
#| include: false

syllable_udf = F.udf(
    lambda t: int(sum(
        len(re.findall(r'[aeiouyàâäéèêëîïôöùûüœ]+', w, re.I))
        for w in (t or "").split()
    )), LongType()
)
type_token_udf = F.udf(
    lambda t: float(len(set((t or "").lower().split())) /
               max(len((t or "").split()), 1)), DoubleType()
)
avg_word_len_udf = F.udf(
    lambda t: float(np.mean([len(w) for w in (t or "").split()])
                    if (t or "").split() else 0.0), DoubleType()
)

stats_sdf = (
    corpus_sdf
    .withColumn("n_chars",     F.length("text"))
    .withColumn("n_words",     F.size(F.split("text", r"\s+")))
    .withColumn("n_sentences", F.size(F.split("text", r"[.!?]+")))
    .withColumn("n_syllables", syllable_udf("text"))
    .withColumn("ttr",         type_token_udf("text"))
    .withColumn("avg_word_len",avg_word_len_udf("text"))
    .drop("text")
)
stats_sdf.cache()

@F.udf(DoubleType())
def udf_fk(nw, ns, nsyl):
    if not nw or not ns or nw == 0 or ns == 0: return 0.0
    return round(206.835 - 1.015*(nw/ns) - 84.6*(nsyl/nw), 3)

@F.udf(DoubleType())
def udf_km(nw, ns, nsyl):
    if not nw or not ns or nw == 0 or ns == 0: return 0.0
    return round(209.835 - 2.048*(nw/ns) - 64.3*(nsyl/nw), 3)

read_sdf = (
    stats_sdf
    .withColumn("fk", udf_fk("n_words","n_sentences","n_syllables"))
    .withColumn("km", udf_km("n_words","n_sentences","n_syllables"))
)
read_sdf.cache()
read_pd = read_sdf.toPandas()
```

```{python}
#| label: word-frequencies
#| include: false

STOPWORDS_FR = {
    "le","la","les","de","du","des","un","une","et","en","a","au","aux",
    "il","elle","ils","elles","je","tu","nous","vous","on","se","sa","son","ses",
    "que","qui","qu","ne","pas","plus","par","sur","dans","avec","est","etait",
    "pour","mais","ou","donc","or","ni","car","ce","cet","cette","ces","mon",
    "ma","mes","ton","ta","tes","lui","leur","leurs","y","si","comme","bien",
    "tout","tous","meme","tres","aussi","toute","toutes","etre","avoir","fait",
    "dit","peut","dont","quel","quelle","quels","quelles","d","l","j","m","n","s","c"
}

words_sdf = (
    corpus_sdf
    .select("filename","title","author","courant",
            F.explode(F.split(F.lower(F.regexp_replace("text", r"[^\w\s]", " ")), r"\s+")).alias("word"))
    .filter(F.length("word") > 2)
    .filter(~F.col("word").isin(STOPWORDS_FR))
    .groupBy("filename","title","author","courant","word")
    .agg(F.count("*").alias("freq"))
)
words_sdf.cache()

w_spec = Window.partitionBy("filename").orderBy(F.desc("freq"))
ranked_sdf = (
    words_sdf
    .withColumn("rank",     F.rank().over(w_spec))
    .withColumn("log_rank", F.log10("rank"))
    .withColumn("log_freq", F.log10(F.col("freq") + 1))
)
ranked_sdf.cache()
zipf_pd = ranked_sdf.filter(F.col("rank") <= 200).toPandas()
zipf_pd["log_rank"] = np.log10(zipf_pd["rank"])
zipf_pd["log_freq"] = np.log10(zipf_pd["freq"] + 1)
```

```{python}
#| label: dialog-segmentation
#| include: false

dialog_udf    = F.udf(lambda t: sum(1 for l in (t or "").split('\n')
                                    if re.match(r'^\s*[«"—–\-]', l)), LongType())
narration_udf = F.udf(lambda t: sum(1 for l in (t or "").split('\n')
                                    if l.strip() and not re.match(r'^\s*[«"—–\-]', l)), LongType())

seg_sdf = (
    corpus_sdf
    .withColumn("dialog_lines",    dialog_udf("text"))
    .withColumn("narration_lines", narration_udf("text"))
    .select("title","author","courant","dialog_lines","narration_lines")
)
seg_sdf.cache()
seg_pd = seg_sdf.toPandas()
seg_pd["total"]      = seg_pd["dialog_lines"] + seg_pd["narration_lines"]
seg_pd["pct_dialog"] = (seg_pd["dialog_lines"] / seg_pd["total"].replace(0, 1) * 100).round(1)
```

```{python}
#| label: sliding-windows
#| include: false

VOWELS = re.compile(r'[aeiouyàâäéèêëîïôöùûüœ]+', re.I)

def sliding_fk(text, window=500, step=250):
    words = text.split()
    out = []
    for i in range(0, len(words) - window, step):
        chunk = words[i:i+window]
        n_s   = max(1, sum(1 for w in chunk if re.search(r'[.!?]$', w)))
        n_syl = sum(len(VOWELS.findall(w)) for w in chunk)
        asl   = window / n_s
        asw   = n_syl  / window
        fk    = round(206.835 - 1.015*asl - 84.6*asw, 2)
        km    = round(209.835 - 2.048*asl - 64.3*asw, 2)
        out.append((i, fk, km))
    return out

sliding_schema = ArrayType(StructType([
    StructField("pos", LongType()),
    StructField("fk",  DoubleType()),
    StructField("km",  DoubleType()),
]))
sliding_udf = F.udf(lambda t: sliding_fk(t, 500, 250) if t else [], sliding_schema)

sliding_sdf = (
    corpus_sdf
    .filter(F.size(F.split("text", r"\s+")) > 40000)
    .select("title","author","courant", sliding_udf("text").alias("wins"))
    .select("title","author","courant", F.explode("wins").alias("w"))
    .select("title","author","courant",
            F.col("w.pos").alias("pos"),
            F.col("w.fk").alias("fk"),
            F.col("w.km").alias("km"))
)
sliding_sdf.cache()
sliding_pd = sliding_sdf.toPandas()
```

```{python}
#| label: zipf-regression
#| include: false

from scipy import stats as scipy_stats

reg_rows = []
for (fname, author, courant, title), grp in zipf_pd.groupby(["filename","author","courant","title"]):
    if len(grp) < 10: continue
    slope, intercept, r, p, se = scipy_stats.linregress(grp["log_rank"], grp["log_freq"])
    reg_rows.append({
        "title":title,"author":author,"courant":courant,
        "slope":round(slope,3),"intercept":round(intercept,3),"r2":round(r**2,3)
    })
reg_df = pd.DataFrame(reg_rows)
```

---

# Analyse Stylométrique du XIXe siècle {background-color="#1E2761"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 1.1em; margin-top: 1em;"}
**Projet 1 · PySpark · Spark NLP**

Krista Carla Sevi · Mick-Wanderly Laveus · Leon Lafosse
:::

::: {style="color: #aaa; font-size: 0.85em; margin-top: 2em;"}
9 auteurs · 2 courants · Romantisme & Réalisme
:::

---

## Plan {background-color="#1E2761"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 1.05em;"}
1. **Description des données et pipeline d'extraction**
2. **Chargement et Transformation**
3. **Pipelines d'annotation Spark NLP**
4. **Analyse Stylométrique**
5. **Expérience Spark et Formats Big Data**
:::

---

# 1 — Description des données {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 0.9em;"}
Pipeline d'extraction · Nettoyage · Corpus
:::

---

## 1 — Taille du corpus par œuvre

```{python}
fig = px.bar(
    read_pd.sort_values("n_words", ascending=True),
    x="n_words", y="title", color="courant",
    color_discrete_map=COLOR_COURANT,
    orientation="h",
    labels={"n_words":"Nombre de mots","title":"Œuvre","courant":"Courant"},
    template="plotly_white",
    height=500,
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_tickformat=",",
    legend_title="Courant",
    margin=dict(l=260, r=20, t=20, b=40),
    font=dict(size=10),
)
fig.show()
```

---

## 1 — Distribution mots & phrases par courant

```{python}
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=["Nombre de mots","Nombre de phrases"])

for courant, color in COLOR_COURANT.items():
    sub = read_pd[read_pd["courant"] == courant]
    fig.add_trace(go.Box(y=sub["n_words"], name=courant,
        marker_color=color, boxmean=True, showlegend=True), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Box(y=sub["n_sentences"], name=courant,
        marker_color=color, boxmean=True, showlegend=False), row=1, col=2)

fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white", height=430,
    legend_title="Courant",
    margin=dict(t=40, b=40),
    font=dict(size=11),
)
fig.show()
```

---

# 2 — Chargement et Transformation {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 0.9em;"}
Statistiques descriptives · Segmentation dialogue/narration
:::

---

## 2 — % de dialogue médian par auteur

```{python}
dial_author = (
    seg_pd.groupby(["author","courant"])["pct_dialog"]
    .median().reset_index()
    .sort_values("pct_dialog", ascending=False)
)

fig = px.bar(
    dial_author, x="author", y="pct_dialog",
    color="courant", color_discrete_map=COLOR_COURANT,
    labels={"pct_dialog":"% de dialogue","author":"Auteur","courant":"Courant"},
    text_auto=".1f",
    template="plotly_white",
    height=430,
)
fig.update_layout(
    legend_title="Courant",
    font=dict(size=11),
    margin=dict(t=20, b=60),
)
fig.show()
```

---

## 2 — Dialogue vs Narration par œuvre (Top 30)

```{python}
seg_sorted = seg_pd.sort_values("pct_dialog", ascending=False).head(30)

fig = px.bar(
    seg_sorted, x="title", y=["dialog_lines","narration_lines"],
    barmode="stack",
    labels={"value":"Lignes","title":"Œuvre","variable":"Type"},
    template="plotly_white", height=430,
    color_discrete_map={"dialog_lines":"#5B8DB8","narration_lines":"#E8A87C"},
)
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=-45, tickfont_size=8)
newnames = {"dialog_lines": "Dialogue", "narration_lines": "Narration"}
fig.for_each_trace(lambda t: t.update(name=newnames.get(t.name, t.name)))
fig.update_layout(legend_title="Type", font=dict(size=10), margin=dict(t=20, b=100))
fig.show()
```

---

# 3 — Pipelines d'annotation Spark NLP {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 0.9em;"}
DocumentAssembler · SentenceDetector · Tokenizer · Normalizer
:::

---

## 3 — Pipeline Spark NLP

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**5 étapes dans l'ordre :**

| # | Annotateur | Rôle |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `DocumentAssembler` | Mise en forme |
| 2 | `SentenceDetector` | Découpage phrases |
| 3 | `Tokenizer` | Tokens |
| 4 | `Normalizer` | Nettoyage |
| 5 | `Normalizer` | Minuscules |

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```python
document  = DocumentAssembler()\
  .setInputCol("text")\
  .setOutputCol("document")

nlp_pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[
  document, sentence,
  tokenizer, normalizer
])

annotated = (
  nlp_pipeline
  .fit(corpus_sdf
    .repartition(N_CORES, "filename"))
  .transform(corpus_sdf)
)
```
:::
:::

---

# 4 — Analyse Stylométrique {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 0.9em;"}
Loi de Zipf · Indices de lisibilité · Distance stylistique
:::

---

## 4 — Loi de Zipf : Romantisme vs Réalisme

```{python}
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=["Romantisme","Réalisme"], shared_yaxes=True)

for col_idx, courant in enumerate(["Romantisme","Réalisme"], start=1):
    sub = zipf_pd[zipf_pd["courant"] == courant]
    seen = set()
    for title, grp in sub.groupby("title"):
        author = grp["author"].iloc[0]
        show = author not in seen
        seen.add(author)
        fig.add_trace(
            go.Scatter(x=grp["log_rank"], y=grp["log_freq"],
                mode="lines", name=author if show else title,
                line=dict(width=1, color=COLOR_AUTHOR.get(author,"#888")),
                opacity=0.6, legendgroup=author, showlegend=show),
            row=1, col=col_idx)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="log10(rang)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="log10(rang)", row=1, col=2)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="log10(fréquence)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", height=430,
    legend_title="Auteur", font=dict(size=11), margin=dict(t=40))
fig.show()
```

---

## 4 — Pente Zipf (alpha) par auteur

```{python}
fig = px.box(
    reg_df, x="author", y="slope",
    color="courant", color_discrete_map=COLOR_COURANT,
    points="all",
    labels={"slope":"Pente alpha","author":"Auteur","courant":"Courant"},
    template="plotly_white", height=430,
)
fig.add_hline(y=-1, line_dash="dash", line_color="grey",
    annotation_text="alpha = -1 (théorique)", annotation_position="right")
fig.update_layout(legend_title="Courant", font=dict(size=11), margin=dict(t=20))
fig.show()
```

---

## 4 — Top-20 mots par auteur (hors stopwords)

```{python}
top20 = (
    words_sdf
    .groupBy("author","courant","word")
    .agg(F.sum("freq").alias("total_freq"))
    .withColumn("rk", F.rank().over(Window.partitionBy("author").orderBy(F.desc("total_freq"))))
    .filter(F.col("rk") <= 20)
    .toPandas()
)

authors = sorted(top20["author"].unique())
ncols   = 3
nrows   = math.ceil(len(authors) / ncols)

fig = make_subplots(rows=nrows, cols=ncols, subplot_titles=authors,
    vertical_spacing=0.08, horizontal_spacing=0.08)

for i, author in enumerate(authors):
    r, c = divmod(i, ncols)
    sub  = top20[top20["author"] == author].sort_values("total_freq")
    courant = sub["courant"].iloc[0]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(x=sub["total_freq"], y=sub["word"], orientation="h",
            marker_color=COLOR_COURANT.get(courant,"#888"),
            name=author, showlegend=False),
        row=r+1, col=c+1)

fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", height=280*nrows,
    font=dict(size=9), margin=dict(t=40))
fig.show()
```

---

## 4 — FK vs KM par œuvre

```{python}
fig = px.scatter(
    read_pd, x="fk", y="km",
    color="courant", symbol="author", text="title",
    size="n_words",
    color_discrete_map=COLOR_COURANT,
    labels={"fk":"Flesch-Kincaid","km":"Kandel-Moles",
            "courant":"Courant","author":"Auteur","n_words":"Nb de mots"},
    template="plotly_white", height=470,
)
fig.update_traces(textposition="top center", textfont_size=7, marker_line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(legend_title="Courant / Auteur", font=dict(size=10), margin=dict(t=20))
fig.show()
```

---

## 4 — Distribution FK / KM par courant

```{python}
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=["Flesch-Kincaid (↑ = plus difficile)","Kandel-Moles (↑ = plus facile)"])

for col_i, col_name in enumerate(["fk","km"], start=1):
    for courant, color in COLOR_COURANT.items():
        sub = read_pd[read_pd["courant"] == courant]
        if sub.empty: continue
        fig.add_trace(
            go.Box(y=sub[col_name], name=courant,
                   marker_color=color, boxmean=True,
                   showlegend=(col_i == 1)),
            row=1, col=col_i)

fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", height=430,
    legend_title="Courant", font=dict(size=11), margin=dict(t=40))
fig.show()
```

---

## 4 — FK glissant : évolution au fil des œuvres

```{python}
for courant in ["Romantisme","Réalisme"]:
    sub = sliding_pd[sliding_pd["courant"] == courant]
    if sub.empty: continue
    fig = px.line(
        sub, x="pos", y="fk", color="title",
        title=f"FK glissant : {courant}",
        labels={"pos":"Position (mots)","fk":"FK","title":"Œuvre"},
        template="plotly_white", height=350,
    )
    fig.update_traces(opacity=0.75, line_width=1.2)
    fig.update_layout(legend_title="Œuvre", font=dict(size=10), margin=dict(t=40))
    fig.show()
```

---

## 4 — Distance stylistique entre auteurs

```{python}
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

style_feats = read_pd.groupby("author")[["fk","km","ttr","avg_word_len"]].mean()
scaler = StandardScaler()
X = scaler.fit_transform(style_feats)

n = len(style_feats)
dist_mat = np.zeros((n, n))
for i in range(n):
    for j in range(n):
        dist_mat[i,j] = np.sqrt(np.sum((X[i]-X[j])**2))

authors_list = list(style_feats.index)
fig = px.imshow(
    dist_mat, x=authors_list, y=authors_list,
    color_continuous_scale="Blues_r",
    labels={"color":"Distance"},
    template="plotly_white", height=460,
    text_auto=".2f",
)
fig.update_layout(font=dict(size=11), margin=dict(t=20))
fig.show()
```

---

# 5 — Expérience Spark & Formats Big Data {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="color: #CADCFC; font-size: 0.9em;"}
Configuration Spark · Shuffles · Benchmark formats
:::

---

## 5 — Shuffles critiques dans le pipeline

```{python}
shuffle_data = pd.DataFrame({
    "Opération": [
        "groupBy(filename, word).agg(count)",
        "rank().over(Window.partitionBy(filename))",
        "write.partitionBy(courant)",
        "Window glissante (sliding_fk)",
        "join(broadcast(df_meta))",
    ],
    "Impact": ["Élevé", "Modéré", "Modéré", "Modéré", "Nul"],
    "Score":  [3, 2, 2, 2, 0],
})

fig = px.bar(
    shuffle_data.sort_values("Score"),
    x="Score", y="Opération",
    color="Impact",
    color_discrete_map={"Élevé":"#d62728","Modéré":"#ff7f0e","Nul":"#2ca02c"},
    orientation="h",
    labels={"Score":"Impact (0=nul, 3=élevé)","Opération":"Opération"},
    template="plotly_white", height=380,
)
fig.update_layout(legend_title="Impact", font=dict(size=11),
    margin=dict(l=320, t=20, b=40))
fig.show()
```

---

## 5 — Benchmark formats de stockage

```{python}
import shutil

fmt_results = []
for fmt in ["parquet","orc","json","csv"]:
    out = str(PARQUET_DIR.parent / f"bench_{fmt}")
    shutil.rmtree(out, ignore_errors=True)

    t0 = time.time()
    if fmt == "csv":
        stats_sdf.write.mode("overwrite").option("header","true").csv(out)
    elif fmt == "json":
        stats_sdf.write.mode("overwrite").json(out)
    elif fmt == "orc":
        stats_sdf.write.mode("overwrite").orc(out)
    else:
        stats_sdf.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out)
    t_write = round(time.time()-t0, 3)

    t0 = time.time()
    if fmt == "csv":
        spark.read.option("header","true").csv(out).count()
    elif fmt == "json":
        spark.read.json(out).count()
    elif fmt == "orc":
        spark.read.orc(out).count()
    else:
        spark.read.parquet(out).count()
    t_read = round(time.time()-t0, 3)

    size_b = sum(
        os.path.getsize(os.path.join(dp, f))
        for dp, _, fs in os.walk(out) for f in fs
    )
    fmt_results.append({
        "Format": fmt.upper(),
        "Ecriture (s)": t_write,
        "Lecture (s)":  t_read,
        "Taille (KB)":  round(size_b/1024, 1),
    })

fmt_df = pd.DataFrame(fmt_results)

fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=3,
    subplot_titles=["Ecriture (s)", "Lecture (s)", "Taille (KB)"])

colors_fmt = {"PARQUET":"#2ca02c","ORC":"#1f77b4","JSON":"#d62728","CSV":"#ff7f0e"}

for col_i, metric in enumerate(["Ecriture (s)", "Lecture (s)", "Taille (KB)"], start=1):
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(x=fmt_df["Format"], y=fmt_df[metric],
            text=fmt_df[metric], textposition="outside",
            marker_color=[colors_fmt.get(f,"#888") for f in fmt_df["Format"]],
            showlegend=False),
        row=1, col=col_i)

fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", height=420,
    font=dict(size=11), margin=dict(t=40))
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| include: false
spark.stop()
```
